sanskrit:
पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनञ्जयः।
पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदरः॥
transliteration:
pāñcajanyaṃ hṛṣīkeśo devadattaṃ dhanañjayaḥ |
pauṇḍraṃ dadhmau mahāśaṅkhaṃ bhīmakarmā vṛkodaraḥ ||
literal_meaning: Krishna (Hṛṣīkeśa) blew Pañcajanya, Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) blew Devadatta, and Bhīma (Vṛkodara), performer of terrifying deeds, blew the great conch Pauṇḍra.
contextual_meaning: At the cliff-edge of war, the Pāṇḍava side asserts collective identity and resolve through a single, synchronized breath. Each conch is an auditory signature of personal history and dharma, turning private virtues into shared courage that momentarily dissolves individual doubt.
psychological_state: A person on the verge of a decisive life-action who momentarily overtops paralysis by anchoring to personal strengths and the felt presence of trusted allies.
trigger_scenario: The night before a whistle-blower delivers evidence that will destroy colleagues’ careers yet protect public safety, gathering with the small team who share the secret and rehearsing the statement together.
anchor_text: Let your shared breath turn private courage into collective resolve.
